summary: Tested role-based portal feature E2E. Backend: 12/12 new role-access pytest tests pass (3 seeded accounts login, cashier RBAC restrictions, store keeper RBAC permissions). Frontend: All 3 quick-login portal cards render and function; Admin lands on / with ADMIN PORTAL + 6 nav items; Cashier lands on /pos with CASHIER PORTAL + 2 nav items (Dashboard, Point of Sale); Store Keeper lands on /inventory with STORE KEEPER + 3 nav items (Dashboard, Inventory, Suppliers). Access restrictions work correctly: Cashier visiting /inventory|/suppliers|/employees|/reports redirects to /pos; Store Keeper visiting /pos|/employees|/reports redirects to /inventory. Manual login with cashier@supermarket.com/cashier123 and storekeeper@supermarket.com/store123 both work and route to correct landing pages. Inventory page shows 24 products / 3 low stock for Store Keeper.

minor.issue: Pre-existing - unauthenticated endpoint accepts arbitrary 'role' (incl. admin). Not part of this iteration but should be locked down.
File now ~1014 lines, even further over the 700-line guideline. Should be split into modules.

action_items: (Optional) Split server.py (1014 lines) into auth.py, products.py, sales.py, reports.py modules.
(Optional) Lock down /api/auth/register to admin-only or restrict role parameter.
critical_code_review_comments: Login.js portalAccounts hardcodes admin/cashier/storekeeper passwords on the public Login page. This is fine for a demo but is a security smell in production - consider feature-flagging behind NODE_ENV !== 'production'.
App.js getRoleDefaultPage and Login.js getRoleLanding are duplicated logic (DRY violation). Extract to a shared util (e.g., src/lib/roles.js).
Layout.js portalConfig falls back to admin theme for unknown roles - silently misclassifies; should display a neutral default.
server.py seed_admin/cashier/storekeeper logic is repeated 3x - extract into a single seed_user(email, password, role, ...) helper.
Cashier role can view /api/products (needed for POS) but the products endpoint exposes cost_price - cashiers should not see margin data. Consider scoping response by role.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@supermarket.com/admin123, Cashier: cashier@supermarket.com/cashier123, Store Keeper: storekeeper@supermarket.com/store123 - all verified via API and UI.
seed_data_creation: test_role_access.py creates and cleans up TEST_SK_<ts> product, TEST_Sup_<ts> supplier, TEST_DelSup supplier (admin cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Role-based features verified at 100%. New tests at /app/backend/tests/test_role_access.py (12 tests). Existing /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py still has 24 tests. Combined 36 backend tests. Quick-login data-testids: quick-login-admin, quick-login-cashier, quick-login-inventory_manager. Sidebar h1 contains role title (ADMIN PORTAL / CASHIER PORTAL / STORE KEEPER). Note: rapid sequential role-switching in playwright can briefly show 0 products before refetch completes - add wait_for_timeout(3-4s) after navigation. Real backend always returns data.
rca of the issue: N/A - all role-based features work as specified.
